headline: Australia Signals Need to Act on Loan Fraud Seen at A$1 Billion
summary: Daniel Mulino spoke at the Australian Financial Review Banking Summit on Monday.  The AFR earlier said a Commonwealth Bank-commissioned report signaled loans based on doctored documents across the wider industry could amount to more than the earlier A$1 billion estimate.  “I suspect that we are not at the bleeding edge,” Mulino said, referring to anti-money laundering laws.
